water.name: South Fork Reservoir
aliases: South Fork of the Humboldt River (Lucky Nugget Subdivision causeway downstream to confluence with the Humboldt River)

speciesGroups.name: Black bass (smallmouth + largemouth, South Fork Reservoir)

sources.title: State of Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners — CR 25-16: Fishing Seasons and Regulations, January 1, 2026 – December 31, 2027
sources.quotedText: South Fork Reservoir and the South Fork of the Humboldt River from the Lucky Nugget Subdivision causeway downstream to its confluence with the Humboldt River: Season: Open year around. Exception: March 1 through June 30, catch and release fishing only for black bass. Limits — Daily: 5 trout, 1 black bass, 1 white bass hybrid (wiper) and 5 channel catfish. Minimum size for black bass and white bass hybrids (wiper) is 15 inches total length. Possession: 10 trout, 2 black bass, 2 white bass hybrid (wiper) and 10 channel catfish. Hours: Any hour of the day or night.

sources.title: South Fork Reservoir — Nevada Department of Wildlife
sources.quotedText: Species present: Rainbow trout, Brown trout, Bowcutt trout/hybrid (rainbow x cutthroat), Smallmouth bass (up to 21 inches), Largemouth bass, Wipers/striped bass hybrids, Channel catfish.

sources.title: Humboldt River, South Fork — Nevada Department of Wildlife
sources.quotedText: Game fish include bowcutt trout (rainbow x cutthroat hybrid), rainbow trout, brown trout, smallmouth bass, and channel catfish.

sources.title: Apply & Buy — Nevada Fishing Licenses — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

parameters.note: Open year-round overall, covering both South Fork Reservoir and the South Fork of the Humboldt River from the Lucky Nugget Subdivision causeway downstream to its confluence with the Humboldt River. Black bass are catch-and-release only March 1 through June 30 (see following season rule).

regulations.humanSummary: South Fork Reservoir and the connecting reach of the South Fork Humboldt River (Lucky Nugget Subdivision causeway downstream to the Humboldt River confluence) are open to fishing year-round.
March 1 through June 30, black bass fishing at South Fork Reservoir is catch-and-release only — no black bass may be harvested or kept during this window.
regulations.verbatimText: Exception: March 1 through June 30, catch and release fishing only for black bass.

regulations.humanSummary: Trout: daily limit 5, possession limit 10, at South Fork Reservoir.

parameters.counting_rule: This harvest limit applies outside the March 1–June 30 catch-and-release window, during which black bass fishing is catch-and-release only (see season rule).

regulations.humanSummary: Black bass (smallmouth and largemouth): daily limit 1, possession limit 2, at South Fork Reservoir (outside the March 1–June 30 catch-and-release period).
White bass hybrid (wiper): daily limit 1, possession limit 2, at South Fork Reservoir.
regulations.verbatimText: 1 white bass hybrid (wiper) ... 2 white bass hybrid (wiper)

regulations.humanSummary: Channel catfish: daily limit 5, possession limit 10, at South Fork Reservoir.

parameters.note: Applies to black bass (smallmouth and largemouth) and white bass hybrid (wiper).

regulations.humanSummary: Minimum size for black bass and white bass hybrid (wiper) is 15 inches total length at South Fork Reservoir.
regulations.verbatimText: Minimum size for black bass and white bass hybrids (wiper) is 15 inches total length.

regulations.humanSummary: Fishing is allowed any hour of the day or night at South Fork Reservoir.
regulations.verbatimText: Hours: Any hour of the day or night.

parameters.note: Standard Nevada resident/nonresident fishing license required for anglers 12 and older.

regulations.humanSummary: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers age 12 and older; no license required under age 12.
regulations.verbatimText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.
